title: ichnocarpus velutinus
intro: Ichnocarpus velutinus, also known as the Velvety Ichnocarp or Poison Nut, is a plant species in the family Apocynaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as potential antiseptic and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and providing protection against infections. In herbal preparations, Ichnocarpus velutinus can be found in the form of powder, oil, or extract, and is often used to treat various health conditions.
